Part 4 — GA4 301 Micro-Application: Integrations & Activation Plan

Author

Asif Kachroo

Published

March 6, 2026

1 Overview

This report documents my completion of the GA4 training module “Dive deeper into GA4 data and reports.” The purpose of this assignment is to demonstrate how GA4 integrates with other tools and how analytics insights can be activated to improve marketing outcomes.

2 1. Course Completion Evidence

I completed the required GA4 training submodules from the Skillshop course “Dive deeper into GA4 data and reports.” These lessons focused on understanding GA4 reports, analyzing user journeys, creating audiences, and using analytics insights to improve marketing performance.

Course Completion Screenshot

3 2. GA4 Integration’s Evidence

The screenshot below shows the Product Links section within the GA4 Admin panel. This area allows Google Analytics to connect with other Google products such as Google Ads, BigQuery, and Search Console. These integration’s allow businesses to combine analytics insights with advertising and data analysis tools.

3.1 Figure 1: Admin → Product Links

4 3. Workflow Diagram

The diagram below shows how data flows from a source into GA4, how insights are generated, and how those insights can lead to actions that improve business outcomes.

Explanation

This workflow illustrates how analytics data moves through the marketing analytics process. Data from a website or application is collected in GA4, where user behavior can be analyzed. From this data, analysts identify insights such as drop-off points or engagement patterns. Businesses can then take action, such as redesigning a landing page or improving the checkout process, which ideally leads to improved performance metrics like higher conversion rates.

5 4. Short Responses

5.1 Integration that would help my CEP

One integration that would help my CEP the most is Google Ads. Connecting GA4 with Google Ads allows marketers to track how advertising campaigns influence user behavior on the website. This connection helps businesses understand which ads drive meaningful actions such as sign-ups or purchases and allows for better campaign optimization.

5.2 Audience for activation

One audience I would create in GA4 would include users who visited important pages such as product or pricing pages and spent significant time on the site but did not complete a purchase or sign-up. These users demonstrate strong interest but did not convert, making them a valuable audience for remarketing campaigns or targeted advertising.

5.3 BigQuery analysis

If BigQuery export were enabled, I would analyze detailed user journeys across multiple sessions using raw event-level data. BigQuery would allow me to run custom SQL queries and identify patterns in user behavior, such as repeated interactions or drop-off points in the funnel. This type of analysis can be difficult to perform using only the standard GA4 interface.

6 Conclusion

GA4 becomes much more powerful when integrated with other tools in the marketing analytics ecosystem. Integrations like Google Ads and BigQuery allow businesses to connect insights from analytics to real marketing actions, helping improve targeting, optimize campaigns, and increase overall performance.
